retrofit_needed.image_fallback_reason: Sprint pipeline runs without gpt-image-1 for state-level pages; hero photo will be added in a downstream batch when US-state visual library is commissioned.

high_concern_flags.note: No hero image generated for this state-level page. Sprint mode: state-level page bundle currently text-only. Schedule image generation in the US-state visual library batch.

Alabama sibling state-topic pages referenced in chip-nav and links-rail don't exist yet (state parent, wages-overtime, workers-comp, new-hire-reporting, employer-registrations, local-occupational-tax, paid-leave-pto, termination-at-will, hiring-guide). Chip-nav entries rendered as anchors mirroring the UK/Germany/France pattern; links-rail US-parent and Alabama-overview rendered as plain text per the link gate. Retrofit when wave-4 US-state mass build-out runs.

Alabama SUTA experience-rated range cited two ways across sources: 0.59 to 6.19 percent (Alabama Department of Revenue official page, 17 steps, contribution only) versus 0.2 to 6.8 percent (third-party payroll providers, stacks special and shared-cost assessments). Drafted prose uses the primary-source 0.59-6.19 range and notes the wider stacked figure in the body. Steen may want a tighter editorial choice on which number to lead with.

United States _shared/child-base.css is a verbatim copy of UK's per the sprint setup. UK CSS contains 4 pre-existing margin-clobber blocks (.pullquote, .callout, .links-rail, .meta-footer). Not introduced by this page; flagged for the UK-wide CSS retrofit when it runs (will fix US simultaneously).
